claim: Context graphs extend RAG by capturing not just data but decision traces, reasoning, and policies that enable agents to retrieve past decisions and apply precedent to new cases.
actionable: Build context graphs that store decision traces (who decided what, why, policy applied) alongside entity data so agents can query precedent and avoid repeating incorrect decisions.
source_title: Why your agents need decision traces, not just documents — Zach Blumenfeld, Neo4j

claim: A knowledge base alone tells agents what the risk factors are; a context graph tells them whether to accept or reject because it includes past decision traces, policies applied, and how similar cases resolved.
actionable: When building agentic systems for high-stakes decisions (finance, healthcare), store not just facts but decision traces and reasoning so agents can cite precedent when making similar decisions.
source_title: Why your agents need decision traces, not just documents — Zach Blumenfeld, Neo4j

claim: Context graphs require three layers: short-term (conversation history), long-term (extracted entities), and reasoning (decision traces); agents lose accuracy without all three.
actionable: Implement agent memory as three distinct graph layers: session conversation, persistent entities (deduplicated and merged), and decision reasoning traces; query all three for decisions.
source_title: Why your agents need decision traces, not just documents — Zach Blumenfeld, Neo4j

claim: Graph embeddings enable hybrid search where semantically similar AND structurally similar decision traces surface together, catching precedent that vector-only search would miss.
actionable: Combine semantic vector search with graph embeddings to find both semantically similar decisions and structurally similar decision patterns when querying precedent.
source_title: Why your agents need decision traces, not just documents — Zach Blumenfeld, Neo4j

claim: Entity extraction from raw text can be automated via spaCy → GliNER → LLM fallback pipeline with deduplication and merging, converting unstructured data into queryable graph entities.
actionable: To populate context graphs from unstructured text, use cascading entity extraction (spaCy for fast patterns, GliNER for learned patterns, LLM fallback for ambiguous cases) followed by deduplication.
source_title: Why your agents need decision traces, not just documents — Zach Blumenfeld, Neo4j
